confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Score of 4 reflects high but not maximum symbolic-order strength: the immediate post-9/11 order achieved strong public cohesion and institutional mobilization, but the unlimited enemy definition and absence of constitutional containment produced instability. Not maximum because the order's strength was fear-driven and began declining within years of the founding moment.

reviewNote: Score value 4/5 defensible: sacred-enemy-escalation mechanism (AUMF's unlimited scope, Bush address framing) supports elevated but not maximum symbolic-order-strength — the constitutional framework survived and eventually provided restraint mechanisms. Confidence 0.60 reflects chain-requires-expansion and the broadening-enemy-definition uncertainty. Hold at score-review until chain expansion to ≥4 passages.
